rationale: Both candidates cover the two addressable ground-truth threads (regression-test coverage and the CuMemTag enum request), but Y's blocker finding is far more precisely grounded: it names five specific pre-existing tests (with line numbers) that call sleep(level=2)->wake_up() and would now break under the new NotImplementedError guard — this is exactly the historical latent gap where the guard was later relaxed after breaking CI (#4905/#4912). X's closest finding (level=2 default bricking the engine) is adjacent but frames it as an API-ergonomics footgun rather than tying it to concrete existing-test/CI breakage, so it doesn't clearly hit the gap. Y's other findings (enum .value redundancy, dead _is_sleeping test helper, level-2 guard applying indiscriminately across stage types) are cited with grep/read evidence and are more verifiable than some of X's speculative points (the 'default' allocator tag assumption, state-desync conjecture).
